rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 10, 2026, line 1. COUNSEL FOR PETITIONER MPS SERVICES, LLC's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. The unopposed motion for withdrawal of attorney of record is granted. The court will sign the order on the appropriate Judicial Council form. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM L. HELTON VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition And Accompanying Document Production Of Plaintiff William L. Helton
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 10, 2026, line 3. DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S Motion To Compel The Deposition And Accompanying Document Production Of Plaintiff William L. Helton. Defendant's unopposed motion to compel the deposition of William L. Helton and the production of documents is granted. The discovery is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010). The parties shall immediately meet and confer regarding the time, place, and manner of the deposition of Mr. Helton. The deposition shall occur within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Plaintiff's objections to the production of documents request lack merit and the court overrules them. Plaintiff shall produce the documents at the time of the deposition. No party requested monetary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: ASTRID RIVERA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Frederick Schrunk Dba Thrillhouse Records
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 10, 2026, line 4. PLAINTIFF ASTRID RIVERA'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Frederick Schrunk dba Thrillhouse Records. The unopposed motion for an order compelling discovery responses and sanctions from defendant Frederick Schrunk dba Thrillhouse Records is granted. Plaintiff served form interrogatories, set one; special interrogatories, set one; and request for production of documents, set one on Schrunk on October 29, 2025. (Aria Decl., Ex. A.) Schrunk has not responded. (Id., para. 7.) Schrunk is ordered to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses by March 30, 2026. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2030.290; id., sec. 2031.300.) Plaintiff seeks sanctions. The court shall impose sanctions against a party who unsuccessfully opposes a motion to compel further responses unless it finds that the opposing party's position had substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of the sanction unjust. (Id., sec. 2030.290, subd. (c); id., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c).) Failure to respond to a discovery motion does not obviate the sanctions request. (Rules of Court, Rule 3.1348, subd. (a).) The court awards $1200 as a reasonable sanction on this motion. Schrunk shall pay the sanction by April 10, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA HARRINGTON ET AL VS. ALEJANDRA GARCIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production, To Compel Production, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 10, 2026, line 6. DEFENDANT ALEJANDRA GARCIA'S Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production, To Compel Production, To Deem All Objections Waived, And For Monetary Sanctions From Plaintiff Linda Harrington And Plaintiffs Counsel Of Record. Defendant Alejandra Garcia's motion to compel objection-free responses to her first set of requests for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff Linda Harrington did not timely respond, has thereby waived objections, did not seek a protective order, and makes no showing that she has provided substantially compliant discovery responses that would enable her, upon a noticed motion, to obtain relief from waiver. Although counsel contends a medical emergency prevented Harrington's response, Harrington provides no competent, nonhearsay evidence of a medical emergency and did not seek a protective order. The evidence before the court casts doubt on the claim of medical emergency. Harrington is ordered to provide complete, verified, objection-free responses to the first set of requests for production by March 30, 2026. Garcia's request for judicial notice is denied. The documents for which counsel seeks judicial notice are not relevant to this motion. Garcia seeks sanctions. The court "shall" award sanctions when a party moves to compel responses to unanswered discovery unless it finds substantial justification or unjust circumstances. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2031.300, subd. (c).) The court does not find unjust circumstances or substantial justification but reduces the sanctions award. A motion to compel responses to unanswered discovery requires a simple showing; Garcia's motion included extraneous material. The court orders Harrington to pay $1500 to Garcia by April 10, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KJELL SPANGBERG ET AL VS. GARY BAUER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion / Discovery calendar for March 10, 2026, line 9. PLAINTIFF KJELL SPANGBERG, AS TRUSTEE, KJELL SPANGBERG'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff Kjell Spangberg's motion for a right to attach order and order for issuance of writ of attachment against defendant Gary Bauer is denied without prejudice, for two reasons. First and most importantly, the court has sustained defendants' demurrer with leave to amend by companion order today. Since there is no present operative complaint, the court cannot say the claim has probable validity. Second, it appears to the court that the probable validity of Spangberg's claim will depend on his evidence that Bauer acknowledged the debt after the limitations period. Spangberg presents some evidence, including in sur-reply papers to which Bauer has had no opportunity to respond. "[T]he hearing on the right-to-attach order has been described as 'similar to a bench trial ....'" (Hobbs, 73 Cal.App.4th at p. 81 [quoting Ahart, Cal. Practice Guide: Enforcing Judgments and Debts 1, 4:4, p. 4-2].) Fairness requires that parties have a full opportunity to present evidence at a right to attach hearing, including evidence responsive to points presented for the first time on reply. (Id. at p. 82.) The court would give Bauer an opportunity to respond to Spangberg's sur-reply evidence in any event. It makes more sense for that opportunity to occur after a First Amended Complaint has been filed. The court therefore denies the motion without prejudice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
